Japan Travel Blog • Free Itineraries, Guides, Tips & Inspiration
# Japan Travel Blog to Help You "Blend In"
Travelling to Japan for the first time, wanting to head back for more, or are[visiting Japan with kids](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/tag/japan-with-kids/)and hope to avoid crowds? Created from over a decade of visits, my comprehensive travel blog to Japanhas a focus on*blending in!*My dozens of articles below explore Japan off the beaten path, authentic cultural experiences, and popular destinations in a sustainable way to ease overtourism.
As one of the first Japan bloggers back in 2017, my obsession and expertise travelling Japan began in 2014. I’veseen tourism change drastically through my regular trips during all seasons, but my heart has always enjoyed seeking uncrowded alternatives to popular spots. I’ve now covered 70% of The Land of the Rising Sun, sharing all my exclusive Japan travel tips with you from my unique perspective!
On this Japan blog you’ll find my personal, tried-and-tested Japan itineraries and travel guides to simplify your Japan trip planning. Find out costs, things to do, where to stay, options to get around and more.Dotted throughout you’ll learn Japanese etiquette, culture and customs to help you better *blend in* to ease the tourism burden and give back to local communities.
I’m always sharing many new Japan travel destinations as I visit up to 4 times annually from Australia, making The Invisible Tourist one of the best Japan travel blogs on the internet for up-to-date information. Whether you’re visiting Japan for cherry blossom season or off-peak beauty,learn how to be an [Invisible Tourist](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/travel-avoid-contributing-to-overtourism-solutions/)when combining the highlights with true hidden gems to make the most of your visit!

## Offline Japan Itinerary PDFs
AD-FREE versions of my Japan itineraries are now available in PDF format for offline use, perfect for when you’re on the go. Be prepared and organised ahead of your future trip!
[
Visit Shop
](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/shop)
#### What to Pack for Japan? 19 Things You Haven’t Thought Of (& What NOT to Bring)
Wondering what things to pack for Japan? My pacing list covers what to wear in Japan (plus what NOT to wear and bring!) It also includes items for how to be a responsible tourist to minimise your environmental footprint as well as things to make the flight more comfortable. Use the handy travel checklist for reference later!
[
Japan Packing List
](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/what-to-pack-for-japan/)
## Discover More of Japan
Whether you’re planning to visit Japan’s Golden Route (Tokyo – Kyoto – Osaka – Hiroshima) or wander off the beaten path, my Japan travel guides cover all the bases. Create your own Japan travel story by exploring lesser-known cities, enjoying cultural experiences and discovering hidden gems to enrich your trip.

#### Planning a trip to Japan? 20+ Essential Travel Tips to Know Before You Go
When is the best time to visit Japan? Just the thought of planning a Japan trip may seem overwhelming at first. For a seemingly small island nation, there is so much to see! Where do you start?
No need to worry as I’ve done all the ground work for you and shared my best advice from my 10+ visits to this incredible country – and not just for autumn color or cherry blossom season.
Find out exactly how to prepare for a trip to Japan, common tourist mistakes, what to book in advance, how to avoid crowds and so much more!
[
Japan Travel Tips
](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/planning-a-trip-to-japan-travel-tips/)

#### Do's and Don'ts in Japan
Japan is an incredible country quite like no other. In saying that, there are a few cultural differences compared to Western countries you may not have thought of!
So what can you do to “be invisible?” How can you not look like a tourist in Japan, avoid contributing to overtourism and make the most of your visit? You don’t have to soley rely on Google Translate…
Learn where to go to escape crowds, discover more about traditional Japanese culture through exciting experiences and more travel tips for your visit!
[
Japanese Etiquette
](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/dos-and-donts-in-japan-tourist-guide-etiquette/)

## Bring Japan to You
![](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/featured_86.jpg)
Like Japanese sweets, snacks and candies? Can’t get to Japan right now but want to satisfy your curiosity? No worries – You’re going to love having them delivered directly to your door, anywhere in the world with TokyoTreat and Sakuraco!
My detailed reviews for these Japanese subscription boxes cover first impressions, notes about delivery, featured snacks and more to help you be an Invisible Tourist at home.

TokyoTreat Review

[
Sakuraco Review
](https://theinvisibletourist.com/sakuraco-review/)
#### Overtourism in Japan
With a boom visitors in recent years, Japan has been facing a host of issues related to overtourism.
The influx of tourists has caused problems with local infrastructure, damage to significant historical sites and had negative impacts on locals’ quality of life.
Many creators are now covering travel to Japan, but The Invisible Tourist was the first, and now one of the few blogs about Japan travel that shareswhat you need to know about overtourism with actionable tips to avoid contributing during your visit.
[
Beat Overtourism
](https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/)
